milestone: A2 (A-b): multi-chunk allocation - a file can fill the whole container, not just chunk 0
status: Passed - FULLY CLEAN (macOS kernel reads a 130 MiB file that spans chunk 0 and chunk 1 byte-for-byte; fsck_apfs reports the container OK with zero warnings after the chunk-1 bitmap was moved to its internal-pool slot, commit 73d24c9 - the earlier cosmetic main-device note is retired).
capability: The COW commit previously allocated only from chunk 0's bitmap, so any container exposed at most ~127 MiB of file data. allocateFsCommitBlocks now spills the data overflow into chunk 1 (findFreeBlocksInBitmapContent over chunk 1's bitmap) and reserves one chunk-0 block for chunk 1's allocation bitmap. The fs-tree/extent-ref records already handle chunk-1 addresses (the file splits into a chunk-0 run + a chunk-1 run via the multi-extent path); applyFileInsertAllocation splits the allocation by chunk (chunk 0's bitmap gets the chunk-0 blocks + the chunk-1 bitmap block; chunk 1's fresh bitmap gets the chunk-1 data) and writeRotatedCib updates both cib entries' free counts + chunk 1's bitmap_addr. Single-chunk commits are byte-identical (chunk1BitmapBlock 0).
host_verify: apfsck -w clean at rest on a 256 MiB (2-chunk) container with a 130 MiB file: chunk 0 free_count 0 (filled), chunk 1 free_count 32044 with bitmap_addr set, total free consistent (chunk 0 + chunk 1 == spaceman main free). 124/124 unit tests pass (single-chunk byte-identical, incl. crash-safety rollback). Per-file payload cap raised 8 MiB -> 256 MiB.

vm_external_gate.environment: qemu/KVM macOS Sequoia recovery; apfs.kext 2332.101.1; the 256 MiB 2-chunk container (volume MCHUNK) attached raw as SATA disk0
method: 1. format MCHUNK 256 MiB (2 chunks) -> commit-image-file-insert big.bin (130 MiB random payload, > chunk 0's ~127 MiB) in a single commit; the data overflows chunk 0 into chunk 1.
2. Apple kernel auto-mounted /Volumes/MCHUNK; `md5 /Volumes/MCHUNK/big.bin` == the host md5 d83f882a10fffe26958c3d302bc6e7b8 (byte-for-byte across the chunk-0 + chunk-1 runs).
3. diskutil unmountDisk force disk1; fsck_apfs -n /dev/rdisk0 -> The container appears to be OK, with one cosmetic 'overallocation detected on Main device (0x7fff+1)' it self-answers no.
vm_external_gate.kernel_behavior: macOS kernel mounted the container read-write, reassembled the 130 MiB file from its chunk-0 and chunk-1 runs byte-for-byte (md5 match), and continued the checkpoint (transaction ID 3 -> 5); the file md5 still matched after the kernel continued the container.
vm_external_gate.apple_fsck_apfs: After the chunk-1 bitmap moved to its IP slot (volume MCIP): Checking the checkpoint with transaction ID 5; the space manager free queue trees clean; the volume appears to be OK; The container /dev/rdisk0 appears to be OK - zero warnings (the earlier overallocation note on the physical bitmap block is gone).
vm_external_gate.evidence_png: apfs-a2-multichunk-ipslot-fsck-CLEAN.png (md5 match + fsck fully clean); apfs-a2-multichunk-130mib-md5-fsck.png (earlier physical-placement run with the cosmetic note)

residual: RESOLVED. The chunk-1 bitmap is now placed in its internal-pool slot (ip_base + 6 = block 191 for a 2-chunk container), as single-CIB containers do, and marked in the sm_ip_bitmap (0x3f -> 0x7f); no chunk-0 block is consumed. fsck_apfs is fully clean (no overallocation note); apfsck clean at rest; chunk-1 bitmap_addr 191, free_count 32045. Only the chunk-1 bitmap's first slot is used; rotating it through its three IP slots on repeated chunk-1 mutations (crash-safe COW) is a future refinement, but a single multi-chunk commit and the kernel's continuation are clean. Containers above 15.75 GiB still need multi-CIB.
gates: clang-format + lizard + cppcheck clean; commits 9e859f9 (multi-chunk allocation), 73d24c9 (chunk-1 bitmap IP slot) - all pre-commit hooks passed, no bypass.
next: Multi-CIB (>15.75 GiB) for the 100 GB image; then the A-b physical-USB flip.
